title: 90-Day Strategic Plan
summary: Build a focused 90-day plan with 3 priorities, measurable outcomes, and weekly check-ins. Stop running without direction.
whenToUseIt: At the start of each quarter, after a pivot, or when you realise you have been busy without being strategic.

steps.title: Audit your current position
steps.description: Write down your revenue, burn rate, team size, top 3 customers, biggest risk, and biggest opportunity. Do not optimise — just describe reality honestly.
steps.tips: If you cannot state your position in 5 sentences, you do not understand it yet.

steps.title: Choose 3 priorities — no more
steps.description: List everything you could work on. Now eliminate. You get 3 priorities for the next 90 days. Everything else is a distraction. A priority must be specific enough to measure and important enough to change the business.
steps.tips: If a priority does not scare you slightly, it is probably not ambitious enough.
steps.aiPrompt: I run [describe business]. My current revenue is [X], burn rate is [Y], team size is [Z]. Here are 10 things I could focus on: [list]. Help me narrow to the 3 highest-leverage priorities for the next 90 days. For each, explain why it matters more than the others.

steps.title: Define measurable outcomes for each priority
steps.description: For each priority, write 1-2 measurable outcomes. Not activities ('launch marketing campaign') but results ('acquire 50 paying customers'). Include a number and a deadline.

steps.title: Break into 30-day milestones
steps.description: Split each 90-day outcome into 3 monthly milestones. Month 1 should be the foundation. Month 2 should be execution. Month 3 should be acceleration and measurement.

steps.title: Set up weekly check-ins
steps.description: Block 30 minutes every Friday to review: What did I commit to this week? What actually happened? What do I commit to next week? What is blocking me? This is non-negotiable.
steps.tips: Do this alone first. Add your team later.

steps.title: Write the plan on one page
steps.description: Put all 3 priorities, their 90-day outcomes, and their monthly milestones on a single page. If it does not fit on one page, it is too complicated. Print it. Put it where you can see it every day.

expectedOutcome: A single-page 90-day plan with 3 clear priorities, measurable outcomes, monthly milestones, and a weekly review habit in place.
commonMistakes: Picking more than 3 priorities — if everything is a priority, nothing is
Writing activity-based goals instead of outcome-based goals
Not reviewing weekly — the plan dies within 2 weeks without check-ins
Making the plan too detailed — complexity kills execution
